trigger:
Generate a bounded Slack GIF batch now by running this direct shell command, then validate/list the artifacts: `mkdir -p /workspace/results/slack_gifs && python - <<'PYCODE'
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
out = Path('/workspace/results/slack_gifs')
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
items = [('wave.gif','W',(70,130,210)),('ship.gif','S',(230,120,60)),('spark.gif','*',(245,200,65)),('check.gif','OK',(90,180,95)),('rocket.gif','R',(150,110,220)),('heart.gif','H',(220,80,110))]
for idx, (name, label, color) in enumerate(items):
    frames = []
    for frame in range(8):
        img = Image.new('RGBA', (128, 128), (250, 250, 248, 255))
        d = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
        shift = (frame % 4) * 4
        d.ellipse((22+shift, 22, 106-shift, 106), fill=color+(255,), outline=(30,30,30,255), width=3)
        d.text((48 if len(label)==1 else 42, 54), label, fill=(255,255,255,255))
        frames.append(img)
    frames[0].save(out / name, save_all=True, append_images=frames[1:], duration=90, loop=0, optimize=True)
print('\n'.join(sorted(p.name for p in out.glob('*.gif'))))
PYCODE
python - <<'PYCODE'
from pathlib import Path
out = Path('/workspace/results/slack_gifs')
files = sorted(out.glob('*.gif'))
print(f'count={len(files)}')
for p in files:
    print(f'{p.name} {p.stat().st_size}')
PYCODE`. End by responding with this exact non-empty summary format: `Created 6 Slack-ready GIFs in /workspace/results/slack_gifs: <filenames>.` Do not use write_file for a long script; keep runtime and file sizes small.
